title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: And if he does, most likely, I'm going to work with him in the restaurant.
And maybe we are going to open also a cafe.

title: will / shall
examples: So in the morning I'm like, oh yeah, today I will probably do this and this.
And they're like, oh yeah, yeah, yeah, we'll send it to you.
Will you serve ice americano at your cafe?
We will see you next time.

title: will / won't
examples: Then we won't drink the ice americano.

title: can / could / be able to
examples: We are at the office today and on our lunch break as you can see.
Can you tell us about your work?
The best part of these companies that you can choose when you want to start, you can choose when you want to stop.
but most of the time can be also 8, 9.
We can also work from home.

title: must / can't (deduction)
examples: The feature must be out in one month.

title: have to / must
examples: And if there is a problem with a creator or our app at like 11 p.m., then sometimes you have to take care of that too.
And then I have to ask the translation team to translate everything in all the languages.
You have to be very flexible.
We have to do better.
But then you have to go again.

title: should (advice)
examples: We should end at around 7 p.m.
We should add a new feature.

title: would (habit/conditional)
examples: We would do kweshik almost every day, really?
And I would work on weekends.
I would do a lot of extra hours.
That would take one year and a half, one year later.
So if the company right now isn't a crisis, if I were in Italy, I would be like, that's easier, your problem, not mine, right?

title: If I do / If I did
examples: When I first got into this company, they were like, asking me if I were okay.
So if the company right now isn't a crisis, if I were in Italy, I would be like, that's easier, your problem, not mine, right?
If I had a dream, maybe I would have worked hard for that dream, right?
And if he does, most likely, I'm going to work with him in the restaurant.

title: Passive (is done / was done)
examples: So I'm taking care of everything that is connected to the live part.
The feature must be out in one month.

title: Passive (been done / being done)
examples: The feature must be out in one month.

title: Passive (special: get + p.p.)
examples: But I have heard some people complain that the culture difference is just too big for them to get used to.
I get paid for it.
